title: Restoration Station: The Biological Imperative of Sleep

content_blocks.title: The Memory Consolidation Journey
steps: Information is initially acquired and processed during wakefulness.
Slow-wave (NREM N3) and REM sleep cycles begin the integration process.
Memories are transferred from the hippocampus to the neocortex for permanent long-term storage.

content_blocks.title: Critical Pillars of Nocturnal Health
points: The glymphatic system actively flushes neurotoxic waste, including amyloid-beta, during deep rest.
Cytokine production peaks during sleep to bolster the immune response against infection.
Sleep serves as a primary regulator for glucose metabolism and insulin sensitivity.

items.a: Active detoxification of brain metabolic byproducts.
items.b: Accumulation of proteins linked to neurodegenerative disease.

items.a: Robust immune response and cytokine release.
items.b: Suppressed immunity and reduced vaccine efficacy.

items.a: Stable hormone regulation and glucose metabolism.
items.b: Increased insulin resistance and risk of type 2 diabetes.
